text:
$GOOGL says Gemini can now create Docs, Sheets, Slides, PDFs and more directly from chat.

Google is turning Gemini from a chatbot into a workspace execution layer.

$AAOI won a ~$21M Texas grant to expand semiconductor and photonics manufacturing in Sugar Land.

The project adds a 210K sq. ft. facility for AI data center transceiver production and is expected to create 500+ jobs.
$INTC UP OVER 10% TODAY  😳

$HOOD CEO Vlad Tenev says company is investing to become a more diversified company even as crypto, options & retail trading still make the business cyclical.

I still think Robinhood is positioned to benefit from rising retail participation and the ~$100T wealth transfer as it scales into a financial services super app.

$NBIS achieved $NVDA Exemplar Cloud certification on GB300 NVL72 validating its AI training performance against Nvidia’s own standards.

Very few cloud providers have this which reinforces Nebius as a real AI infrastructure player and not just another GPU rental platform.

SpaceX’s board reportedly approved a new Elon Musk pay package ahead of its IPO tied to a $7.5T valuation, a 1M-person Mars colony & space-based data centers.

Musk gets zero shares unless the milestones hit & filings show he can only be fired from SpaceX if he fires himself.

AWS CEO Matt Garman said $AMZN has about 7 GW of capacity committed to OpenAI and Anthropic over the next several years.

AWS is prioritizing customers that strengthen its AI platform even if that means taking less revenue near term to build more value long term.

$TMDX is investing in Germany-based PAD Aviation to build Europe’s first dedicated transplant logistics network.

This is the first step toward replicating its U.S. NOP model in Europe by pairing OCS technology with dedicated air and ground logistics.

$V up ~9% as the market realizes AI agents aren't replacing Visa’s global payments network.

I guess “agentic commerce” still requires commerce.

$SOFI member growth has now reached 14.7M with management guiding for at least 30% CAGR through 2028.

The company is positioning itself as the digital on-ramp where banking, investing and digital assets converge.
$BE UP 25% AND JUST HIT NEW ALL-TIME HIGHS

$TSM has sold its remaining stake in $ARM.

TSMC Partners sold ~1.1M Arm shares at an average price of $208 totaling roughly $231M.

$BE just reported its fastest YoY revenue growth (+57% YoY) in nearly seven years.

The company is becoming one of the most strategically positioned behind-the-meter power plays in the AI infrastructure buildout.

$SOFI just posted its fastest revenue growth in nearly three years (up 41% YoY) with demand still showing up across the platform.

Management pushed back on two key bear arguments saying last year’s capital raises were not dilutive to tangible book value per share and partner demand (LPB) for SoFi’s loans exceeded what the company chose to fulfill.

This is what happens when $AMZN, $GOOGL, $META & $MSFT all report in the same afternoon.

The market doesn't care about my Oura readiness score  😅
$SOFI down over 8% despite a revenue beat and in-line EPS as the market wanted a guidance raise that didn’t come.

Last week, the UAE asked for swap lines with the Fed/Treasury.

This week, they leave OPEC+.

While the world asks who will blink first, the US or Iran ... maybe the answer is the UAE.

The economic pain the UAE is under is intolerable for them, worse than that of Iran.

Meta recently signed a multi-billion dollar deal with Amazon to access tens of millions of Graviton CPU cores for agentic AI workloads, with Amazon saying this will make Meta one of the largest global Graviton customers. 

$META $AMZN $ARM

📺 MEMORY STOCKS ARE EXPLODING & WHY I’M STILL LONG $NVDA

Memory stocks are exploding after $STX delivered a blowout quarter, triggering massive upside across names like $MU and $SNDK.

This group has been trend-perfect — higher highs, respecting moving averages, strong earnings follow-through.

If you’ve been shorting this group based on “valuation” or macro opinions, you’ve likely been run over. If you’ve been trading the trend (following price + MAs), you’ve made serious money.

*

$NVDA remains a core long, supported by strong price action and potential upside from rising Big Tech CapEx tonight. 

Higher CapEx = more AI infrastructure spending → more demand for chips → bullish for $NVDA BUT also semis $SMH.

Tonight's earnings from $AMZN $META $GOOGL and $MSFT are critical for both memory and semis, so watch them closely.

Markets are mixed ahead of today's #FOMC. Prediction markets see zero movement for the remaining 2026 meetings. Strong earnings are lifting tech, with $STX hitting new highs and boosting S&P names. Tonight marks peak earnings-know what you own.

Today is likely Jerome Powell’s last FOMC meeting as Fed chair

Kevin Warsh is set to take over next.

At the same time, Polymarket is pricing in 0 rate cuts this year, even with five meetings still left on the calendar.

Nasdaq 100 $QQQ reaction on FOMC days over the past year:

🟩 MAY: +0.40% (Pause)
🟥 JUN: -0.01% (Pause)
🟩 JUL: +0.14% (Pause)
🟥 SEP: -0.21% (25bps Cut)
🟩 OCT: +0.48% (25bps Cut)
🟩 DEC: +0.65% (25bps Cut)
🟥 JAN: -0.02% (Pause)
🟥 MAR: -1.43% (Pause)
